pattern: Whenever a running prefix sum hits a value you've seen target-away before, greedily cut a subarray there and forget everything before the cut -- a later, shorter subarray never blocks more future subarrays than an earlier one would.
hints: Track a running prefix sum and a set of prefix sums seen so far (starting with 0 before the array begins).
At each element, check whether (current prefix sum - target) is in that set -- if so, you've just completed a subarray summing to target ending here.
Take it greedily: count it, then wipe the seen-set and restart the prefix sum from this point, because ending the subarray as early as possible only leaves more room for subarrays after it.

edge_inputs.description: five overlapping candidates, only two can be taken without overlap
negative numbers mixed in, picking non-adjacent windows
single element not equal to target
target is zero, some elements are individually zero
greedy must take the earliest ending match, not a longer one that blocks a later match
no subarray ever sums to target

stress.description: random small-range integers with a random small target to create many overlapping candidates
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(-5, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randint(0, 6)
    return [nums, target]
